hero.description: Full Stack Developer specialized in multi-tenant SaaS with native AI. Building <highlight>Sellorex</highlight> · B2B SaaS with 107 models, 1036 endpoints, 12 AI agents orchestrated via multi-provider gateway with fallback. Plus the <highlight>Pulse Ecosystem</highlight> · 4 production-ready SaaS, 380+ tests, 740+ commits.
hero.descriptionText: · B2B SaaS with 107 models, 1036 endpoints, 12 AI agents orchestrated via multi-provider gateway with fallback.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: I build what I operate · I operate what I build. Every technical decision becomes a responsibility when something breaks in production.
about.personalOpener: I didn't get here in a straight line · every SaaS I built started from a pain I felt first myself. I learned that code that scales needs architecture, not just logic · that whoever designs also has to operate · that every product is just a promise until it runs in production, with real users complaining or loving it.
about.personalDrives: I build to last · not to present. Every technical decision I make is meant to run when I'm not watching. That means observability inside the code (not after), failure modes mapped before the happy path, and schema discipline before any endpoint. I don't outsource architectural decisions · I design, implement, operate.
about.heading: End-to-end engineering · from design system to
about.paragraph1: Full Stack Developer focused on multi-tenant SaaS with native AI in production. Real end-to-end ownership: frontend, backend, database with vector extensions (pgvector + HNSW), distributed cache (Redis), queues (BullMQ), real-time (Socket.io), structured observability, security and CI/CD. I don't outsource architectural decisions · I design, implement and operate.
about.paragraph2suffix: · B2B SaaS with native AI: 107 relational models, 1036 REST endpoints, end-to-end multi-tenant with Row-Level Security, 12 specialized agents (orchestrator + 11 business + super admin with 227 tools across 43 categories) coordinated via Event Bus pub/sub in 10 Worlds. Multi-provider LLM gateway with automatic fallback, 5-layer anti-hallucination, 3-tier Budget Enforcement, hierarchical Memory System with semantic deduplication.
about.paragraph3suffix: · 4 production-ready apps sharing a design system, 380+ tests, 740+ commits · Pulse Theme already commercially published. Each app live in production · zero abandoned code in portfolio.
about.manifesto: AI as infrastructure · not as wrapper
about.manifestoQuote: I build the gateway, not the wrapper.
about.bioPrefix: Multi-tenant SaaS with AI treated as infrastructure · I design systems that scale.
about.bioMiddle: · 126 models, 1036 endpoints, 12 agents orchestrated through a gateway with automatic fallback.
about.bioSuffix: · 4 apps in production, Pulse Theme commercially published. End-to-end ownership · I design, ship, and operate.

idempotency.question: API idempotency · what for?
idempotency.tldr: Same operation repeated has same effect · makes retry safe without duplication
idempotency.detail: GET, PUT, DELETE are idempotent by design · POST is not. Real risk: client retries after timeout, charges twice. Solution: Idempotency-Key header (UUID generated by client), server stores response of first call and returns the same without re-executing. Stripe and PayPal use exactly this pattern. In queue workers (at least once delivery): UPSERT instead of INSERT, or check job_id in DB before processing.
idempotency.proof: Sellorex /charge uses Idempotency-Key · Stripe webhook can fire 2× without double-charge

cap.question: CAP theorem · how to decide?
cap.tldr: Distributed system picks 2 of 3 · Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance
cap.detail: In practice, network partitions will happen · real choice is CP or AP. CP (Postgres, MongoDB with majority): sacrifices availability during partition · refuses writes to keep consistency. AP (DynamoDB, Cassandra): accepts writes on any node, resolves conflicts later. BASE = Basically Available, Soft state, Eventual consistency. Criterion: financial data → CP · feed/timeline → AP.
cap.proof: Sellorex DB → CP (multi-tenant consistency critical) · Redis cache → AP (stays available even in split-brain)

saga.question: Saga pattern · distributed transaction?
saga.tldr: Sequence of local steps with compensations · solves cross-service ACID
saga.detail: In microservices you can't have ACID cross-DB. Saga: each service runs a local step + emits event. If it fails midway, run compensating actions (undo) on previous steps. Two styles: choreography (services coordinate via events · low coupling, hard debug) or orchestration (central orchestrator commands · easy debug, single point of failure).
saga.proof: Sellorex onboarding · create tenant → reserve quota → charge card → activate worlds · failure on any step triggers full rollback

coreWebVitals.question: Core Web Vitals · what to measure?
coreWebVitals.tldr: LCP (loading), INP (interactivity), CLS (visual stability) · Google's targets
coreWebVitals.detail: LCP: time to largest visible element · target <2.5s. INP (replaced FID in 2024): worst interaction latency · target <200ms. CLS: layout shift during load · target <0.1. Google uses in SEO ranking. Lighthouse measures in lab (synthetic) · RUM (Real User Monitoring) measures real users with web-vitals.js. Gap is large · always validate with RUM before declaring optimized.
coreWebVitals.proof: Every Sellorex frontend PR · checks Lighthouse before merge · LCP <1.8s, CLS <0.05 in prod

codeSplitting.question: Code splitting · how to apply?
codeSplitting.tldr: Splits bundle into smaller chunks · loads on demand · 5× smaller initial load
codeSplitting.detail: By route: const Page = lazy(() => import('./Page')) + Suspense fallback. By component: heavy Modal becomes lazy. By library: charts (Recharts), editor (Monaco), markdown · only load when user uses it. Webpack/Vite/Turbopack splits automatically on dynamic imports. Watch out for waterfall: lazy inside lazy increases TTI · prefetch on hover helps.
codeSplitting.proof: Pulse Finance Dashboard with 50 charts · code split per tab reduced initial bundle by 60%

memoization.question: Memoization · when does it pay off?
memoization.tldr: When recomputation is expensive AND runs many times · otherwise it's overhead
memoization.detail: useMemo: expensive derived computation from props/state (10k item filter, chart aggregation). useCallback: stable function reference (deps of memoized child, or effect deps). React.memo: pure component with stable props (card list, ItemRow). Anti-pattern: memoize everything "just in case" · costs CPU comparing deps every render. Measure first with Profiler, optimize after.
memoization.proof: Sellorex Sentinel dashboard · useMemo on chart aggregator (200k events) · render time 320ms → 18ms

jwtStateless.question: JWT stateless · ok for production?
jwtStateless.tldr: Yes, but with trade-off · stolen token is valid until it expires
jwtStateless.detail: JWT = Header.Payload.Signature in base64. Server only verifies signature · no DB hit per request (real stateless). Gotchas: long-lived token (1h+) is risky if leaked · use short-lived (15min) + refresh token. Refresh token: stored in httpOnly+SameSite cookie + rotation per use + invalidation via blacklist (needs state · honest trade-off). Instant cross-device logout requires session in DB.
jwtStateless.proof: Sellorex API · 15min access token in memory + refresh in httpOnly cookie · logout invalidates refresh via Redis blacklist

oauth2.question: OAuth2 · 4 grant types · which to use?
oauth2.tldr: Authorization Code + PKCE (web/mobile), Client Credentials (server-to-server), Device, Refresh
oauth2.detail: Authorization Code + PKCE: current standard for web and mobile apps · interception-proof without needing client secret. Client Credentials: backend calling backend (no user · no browser). Device: TVs, IoT (shows code to log in via phone). Refresh Token: renews access token without re-login. Implicit grant and Resource Owner Password grants are deprecated · don't use.
oauth2.proof: Sellorex login with Google/GitHub · Authorization Code + PKCE · Sentinel calls external API via Client Credentials

passwordStorage.question: Password · how to store in 2026?
passwordStorage.tldr: argon2id (preferred) or bcrypt cost 12+ · NEVER MD5/SHA
passwordStorage.detail: argon2id: winner of the Password Hashing Competition · GPU/ASIC resistant · modern standard. bcrypt: still solid with work factor 12+ (rotate up as hardware improves). Auto salt included in final hash. Critical gotchas: NEVER SHA/MD5 (too fast · trivial brute force), NEVER log passwords in audit trails, always HTTPS in transport, always rate limit /login to prevent credential stuffing.
passwordStorage.proof: Sellorex signup · argon2id with memory=64MB, iterations=3, parallelism=4 · stores hash only · zero passwords in logs

understand.title: Failure modes before happy path
understand.description: What breaks at 100x scale, cross-tenant, partial deploy · real constraints surfaced before any line of code.

architect.title: Schema before code
architect.description: Invariants, composite indexes (tenantId, createdAt) · isolation enforced in one layer · pub/sub and replaceability from commit 1.

ship.title: Production-ready from commit 1

iterate.title: Operating is part of building

patterns.intro: 30 patterns auditable in the Sellorex codebase · each one implemented and in production. A recruiter can request file and line number · it's all there.

multitenancy.title: Multi-tenancy
multitenancy.item1: tenantId filter on every query (auto-injected via Prisma extension)
multitenancy.item2: JWT carries tenantId · NEVER trust the request body
multitenancy.item3: 152 composite indexes with tenantId on critical models
multitenancy.item4: Middleware chain: cors → helmet → auth → tenant → rateLimit → handler
multitenancy.item5: INTERNAL_ADMIN role for cross-tenant ops with audit trail

resilience.title: Resilience
resilience.item2: Multi-provider fallback chain (Claude → OpenAI → Qwen → DeepSeek)
resilience.item3: BullMQ retry with exponential backoff + dead letter queue
resilience.item5: Timeouts + abort controllers on every external call

security.title: Security
security.item3: RBAC: OWNER, ADMIN, MANAGER, MEMBER, VIEWER, INTERNAL_ADMIN
security.item4: Bcrypt saltRounds: 10 + password never logged or audited
security.item5: Webhook signature HMAC-SHA256 (Stripe SDK + HeyGen + BytePlus)

observability.title: Observability
observability.item3: AiUsageLog · cost per request, tokens, model, latency
observability.item5: ErrorLog persisted in DB for alerting + retrospectives

dataLayer.title: Data Layer
dataLayer.item2: PostgreSQL 18.3 + pgvector v0.8.2 + HNSW for semantic search
dataLayer.item3: Transactions via prisma.$transaction() on atomic operations
dataLayer.item4: Auto-migration via ensureSchemaSync() at boot (Nixpacks Railway)
dataLayer.item5: Each SQL idempotent with IF NOT EXISTS (Postgres error 42601)

cacheStrategy.title: Cache Strategy
cacheStrategy.item1: L1 in-memory cache (credential vault: memory → DB → env)
cacheStrategy.item2: L2 Redis (ioredis) for sessions + dashboards + queues
cacheStrategy.item3: BullMQ Redis-backed queues with concurrent workers
cacheStrategy.item4: Cache keys namespaced by tenantId · zero cross-tenant leak

codeSnippet.caption: 5-layer pipeline with tenant isolation, 3-tier budget enforcement, automatic multi-provider fallback and structured observability · real production code from Sellorex.

snippets.gateway: 5-layer pipeline · Zod validation, routing by complexity and plan, 3-tier budget enforcement, multi-provider fallback, and structured observability per call.
snippets.tenant: Multi-tenant isolation at middleware · Prisma extension auto-injects tenantId in every query. Cross-tenant leaks are architecturally impossible.
snippets.antiHallucination: Anti-hallucination · 5 mandatory layers before any agent output reaches the user. Schema, fact-check, tool whitelist, tenant boundary, semantic similarity.
snippets.fallback: Real resilience · 4 providers chained (Claude → OpenAI → Qwen → DeepSeek) with circuit breaker and exponential backoff. A down provider doesn't take the system with it.
snippets.eventBus: Typed pub/sub Event Bus · 9 worlds coordinate via events with zero coupling. Adding a new capability is just subscribing · no cross-world refactor required.
snippets.ensureSchemaSync: Boot-time auto-migration · Nixpacks (Railway) doesn't run migrations. Each SQL is idempotent with IF NOT EXISTS · 30+ ALTERs split apart to avoid Postgres error 42601.
snippets.idempotency: Real idempotency · Stripe webhooks can fire twice. processedWebhook table + transaction make double-charges architecturally impossible.

multitenant.title: Multi-tenant SaaS correct from day 1
multitenant.bullet1: Schema-level enforcement · tenantId in 95 of 126 models · 3 nullable are system-level by design (cross-tenant audit logs)
multitenant.bullet2: JWT is source of truth · request body NEVER decides isolation · classic parameter pollution attack surface
multitenant.bullet3: Prisma extension $extends({query}) auto-injects tenantId on every query · 1 enforcement point · zero per-endpoint boilerplate
multitenant.bullet4: AsyncLocalStorage for context propagation · tenantId available in any async function without prop drilling
multitenant.bullet5: 152 composite indexes with tenantId · 22 are (tenantId, createdAt) on hot-path · Claude agent auditor scans queries missing the filter

aiGateway.title: AI Gateway with cost intelligence
aiGateway.bullet1: Single entry point · every LLM call passes through gatewayCall() · zero SDK called directly · audit, cost and rate-limit in 1 place
aiGateway.bullet2: Cost calculator with Math.ceil in micro-USD · 10M calls × cent fraction = $5 lost if truncated · avoids accounting drift at scale
aiGateway.bullet3: Smart routing · analyzeComplexity() + tenantPlan decide between Claude Sonnet 4.5 / Haiku 4.5 / Opus 4.7 · routingReason persisted for audit
aiGateway.bullet4: Claude API with streaming first-class + native tool use + extended thinking · graceful degradation engine keeps uptime even if Anthropic fails
aiGateway.bullet5: AiUsageLog feedback loop · persists tokens, cost, latency, complexityScore + confidenceScore + feedbackScore · router self-improves with real prod signals

antiHallucination.title: AI that never invents data in production
antiHallucination.bullet1: Never invents data · says "I don't have data on X. Want me to fetch?" instead of fictional numbers
antiHallucination.bullet2: Cites the source for every number/name/fact · tool result, system context, or marked general knowledge
antiHallucination.bullet3: Differentiates fact · trend · suggestion · never mixes them without flagging
antiHallucination.bullet4: Empty result is information · doesn't invent alternatives · says "not found · possible reasons"
antiHallucination.bullet5: Names and IDs are sacred · never invents leads, deals, companies, or IDs

modular.title: Modular Event-Driven Architecture
modular.bullet1: Modular per domain · modules/{name}/ with routes/service/repository/types · each module replaceable · new feature = new folder
modular.bullet2: Imports always via absolute path (@/) · zero relative-hell · cross-module refactor trivial · IDE goto-definition consistent
modular.bullet3: Typed Event Bus pub/sub · 1 event → N independent listeners · isolated failures · payload carries tenantId
modular.bullet4: Adding new capability = just subscribe · zero cross-team coordination · new module reads existing events without asking for refactor
modular.bullet5: Tenant isolation in 1 layer · Repository injects tenantId on every query · Claude agent auditor flags violations before security review

infra.title: EnsureSchemaSync() · idempotent boot migrations
infra.bullet1: Nixpacks (Railway) doesn't run prisma migrate deploy · every schema change becomes idempotent SQL at boot
infra.bullet2: IF NOT EXISTS on everything · runs on EVERY boot · NOOP if exists, applies if not · zero downtime
infra.bullet3: Each SQL as individual $executeRawUnsafe · multiple in one call = Postgres error 42601
infra.bullet4: .catch(() => {}) on each call · silent failures don't block API boot
infra.bullet5: 90 idempotent statements running today · ALTER TABLE / ADD COLUMN / ALTER TYPE / DROP NOT NULL

idempotency.title: Architectural idempotency
idempotency.bullet1: GET/PUT/DELETE are idempotent per HTTP spec · POST never · solution: Idempotency-Key header (client UUID)
idempotency.bullet2: Race condition of 2 simultaneous requests · INSERT ON CONFLICT + advisory lock · only 1 wins, other returns cached response
idempotency.bullet3: 24h TTL on keys (Stripe/PayPal pattern) · cron clears expired · retry after 24h is not idempotent
idempotency.bullet4: Saga pattern · order → stock → card → shipment · failure triggers compensating actions (undo) · no global rollback
idempotency.bullet5: Outbox pattern · publish event + persist DB in SAME transaction · exactly-once even with network partition

multitenant.title: Multi-tenant correct from day 1
multitenant.description: Repository pattern injects tenantId in every query · automated auditor scans the codebase · Row-Level Security backup in pgvector · zero cross-tenant leaks in 6 months of production.

aiGateway.title: AI Gateway with cost intelligence
aiGateway.description: 5-layer pipeline · 4 chained providers (Claude/OpenAI/Qwen/DeepSeek) with automatic fallback · 3-tier budget enforcement · Math.ceil on cent fractions prevents loss at scale.

sentinel.title: Sentinel · self-monitoring SaaS
sentinel.description: 263 admin tools across 43 categories · detects anomalies, slow queries, regressions, churn risk · proposes fixes via PowerRequest · auto-tunes thresholds via P95 baseline.

sellMode.title: Sell Mode · self-selling SaaS
sellMode.description: 12 coordinated growth engines · outbound, closer, demo-gen, nurture, content, capture · the platform runs marketing/sales 24/7 with no human intervention.

modular.title: Modular event-driven architecture
modular.description: 9 independent Worlds coordinated via typed Business Event Bus · 32 modules with barrel index · new feature = new folder · add capability without touching anything else.

quality.title: Quality as standard, not feature
quality.description: TypeScript strict mode (zero unjustified any across ~330k LOC) · Zod runtime on every input · ADRs for every decision · conventional commits · mandatory CI/CD.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Each project is a complete case study · from problem to solution, with documented architecture, tests and automated deploy

pulseDs.description: Production-grade design system with Atomic Design, 25 dashboard variants, custom SVG animations and full accessibility with Radix UI.
pulseDs.problem: Repeated components in every project, with no visual consistency or real reuse.
pulseDs.solution: Unified design system with tokens, primitives and patterns that powers all SaaS apps in the ecosystem.

pulseChat.description: Full-stack chat with WebSocket, 32 typed events, offline queue with exponential retry and 40+ components adapted from the Design System.
pulseChat.problem: Going beyond CRUD · real-time communication with concurrent state and resilience.
pulseChat.solution: Event-driven architecture with end-to-end type safety and automatic recovery mechanisms.

pulseFinance.description: Multi-tenant financial SaaS with Clean Architecture, type-safe Hono API, Redis cache and background jobs with BullMQ.
pulseFinance.problem: Demonstrate production-ready architecture with multi-tenancy, cache and async processing.
pulseFinance.solution: Clean Architecture with defined layers, Redis for performance and BullMQ for heavy jobs.

timeline.title: My
timeline.subtitle: Self-taught by choice, Full Stack by dedication · every course became a project, every project became a product, and consistency did the rest

start.title: The First Step
start.description: I decided to learn web development on my own. No bootcamp, no shortcuts · I started from the basics because solid fundamentals are what separate those who code from those who solve problems.
achievements: HTML, CSS and JavaScript from scratch
First contact with React and TypeScript

courses.title: Continuous Learning
courses.description: I invested time and resources in courses to accelerate learning and fill technical gaps. Each course was a stepping stone · real knowledge solidified when I started applying everything in my own projects.

practice.title: Practice Projects
practice.description: Theory without practice is half-knowledge. I built dozens of small projects to solidify each concept: REST APIs, authentication, CRUD, database integration.
achievements: REST APIs with Express and Prisma

pulseDs.title: Pulse Design System
pulseDs.description: With a solid foundation built, I wanted to go beyond study projects. I created a complete design system from scratch · not a copied library, but 100+ components designed to scale.
achievements: i18n with pathname routing in 3 languages

pulseChat.title: Pulse Chat · Real-Time
pulseChat.description: Full-stack real-time app from the ecosystem. WebSocket with 32 typed events, offline queue and 98 tests. Solving real-time problems in production taught more than any course.

pulseFinance.title: Pulse Finance · Clean Architecture
pulseFinance.description: Multi-tenant financial dashboard with real clean architecture. Well-defined layers, Redis cache, background jobs · the level of complexity that separates study projects from real products.

ecosystem.title: Complete Pulse Ecosystem
ecosystem.description: The projects connected into a real ecosystem. Shared design tokens, 380+ tests, CI/CD across all repos. It's not just code · it's architecture that scales.
achievements: SaaS apps connected through design tokens
CI/CD with GitHub Actions across all repos
